scenario: At Forge Retail, an on-call engineer inspects a Camunda 8.8 order process with 3500 instances stuck on the same condition expression. Operate shows: Expected to evaluate condition 'orderValue>=100' successfully, but failed because: Cannot compare values of different types: STRING and INTEGER. The engineer wants to clear the incident on one instance before scripting a batch.
question: Which sequence of actions on the instance page resolves this incident according to the Operate tutorial?

options.text: Click the edit icon next to orderValue, remove the quotation marks from the value, click the checkmark icon to save, then initiate a retry from the top right or the Operations area
Click the edit icon next to orderValue, wrap the value in additional quotation marks, click save, then call the retry endpoint from the Tasklist application for the same instance
Open Modeler, change the gateway condition to compare strings instead of integers, redeploy the process, and the affected instances retry automatically once the new version is live
Cancel the affected instance from the instance page, then create a new instance with orderValue set as an integer using zbctl so the condition can evaluate successfully

a.text: Correct. The tutorial fixes the type-mismatch incident by editing orderValue to remove the quotation marks (making it an integer), saving with the checkmark, and then starting a retry from the top right or the Operations area of the instance page.

b.text: Incorrect. Adding more quotation marks keeps the value as a string, which is what caused the type-mismatch in the first place; the fix is to remove the quotes so the value is an integer.

c.text: Incorrect. Redeploying a model creates a new version, and existing instances continue on their original version; the tutorial resolves the incident by editing the variable on the running instance and retrying.

d.text: Incorrect. Cancelling and recreating is not the documented fix; Operate supports editing the variable on the existing instance and triggering a retry, preserving the original instance and its history.

explanation: The example incident is a type mismatch where orderValue is a STRING but the condition expects INTEGER. The documented resolution is to edit orderValue, remove the quotation marks so it becomes an integer, save with the checkmark, then retry the instance from the top right or the Operations area.
